title: Jesus love for his father part 3
scripture_text: ‘’After Jesus said this, he looked toward heaven and prayed: “Father, the hour has come. Glorify your Son, that your Son may glorify you. For you granted him authority over all people that he might give eternal life to all those you have given him. Now this is eternal life: that they know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ, whom you have sent. I have brought you glory on earth by finishing the work you gave me to do. And now, Father, glorify me in your presence with the glory I had with you before the world began.’’
devotional:
3. Jesus lived with a sense of being sent.

In verse 3 we read ‘’Now this is eternal life: that they know you the only true God, and Jesus Christ, whom you have sent.’’

Six times in this chapter Jesus mentions the fact he has been sent. In fact thirty-eight times in John’s gospel we read about "him who sent me." It is a constant theme. The implications for Jesus are clear. He does not come as an independent agent with his own message. Being sent, meant that Jesus was submissive not independent. Jesus’ identity related to his awareness of being- sent.

Let me try to explain the implications of what it means to be sent. Jesus never lived without a sense of direction or purpose, he never lived according to his own agenda, as the Son of God he teaches us that the life of a child of God is a ‘sent,’ life, a life of submission to Gods appointed commission and purpose.

Sometimes when we make our lives all about us and our agenda, the very act of selfish living robs us of significant purpose. As a Son, Jesus understood that his life was not about him, but his Father

In John 20:21 Jesus said, ‘As the father sent me so I send you.’ in other words, we have been called into a life that is as submissive as Jesus.’ To live life as if we have not been sent is to miss an authentic spiritual vocation, reducing our lives to an economic cog in a commercial system, that only exists to generate income.

Do not misunderstand what is being said here, we are not talking about being sent as if it were some big emotional experience where you are called to some ministry or mission location. Rather, being sent is the natural DNA of every child of God, it is living with a continual sense of God’s call upon your life irrespective of the circumstances you may find yourself in. So if you are young or old, male or female, employed or retired, married or single, rich or poor, healthy or ill, if you are a child of God, then you have be sent into your world to continue the mission of Jesus.
prayer: Father may my life be a continuation of the life and ministry of Jesus.
html:
Jesus love for his father part 3

Study Text: John 17 v 1-5
‘’After Jesus said this, he looked toward heaven and prayed: “Father, the hour has come. Glorify your Son, that your Son may glorify you. For you granted him authority over all people that he might give eternal life to all those you have given him. Now this is eternal life: that they know you, the only true God, and Jesus Christ, whom you have sent. I have brought you glory on earth by finishing the work you gave me to do. And now, Father, glorify me in your presence with the glory I had with you before the world began.’’

3. Jesus lived with a sense of being sent.

In verse 3 we read ‘’Now this is eternal life: that they know you the only true God, and Jesus Christ, whom you have sent.’’

Six times in this chapter Jesus mentions the fact he has been sent. In fact thirty-eight times in John’s gospel we read about "him who sent me." It is a constant theme. The implications for Jesus are clear. He does not come as an independent agent with his own message. Being sent, meant that Jesus was submissive not independent. Jesus’ identity related to his awareness of being- sent.

Let me try to explain the implications of what it means to be sent. Jesus never lived without a sense of direction or purpose, he never lived according to his own agenda, as the Son of God he teaches us that the life of a child of God is a ‘sent,’ life, a life of submission to Gods appointed commission and purpose.

Sometimes when we make our lives all about us and our agenda, the very act of selfish living robs us of significant purpose. As a Son, Jesus understood that his life was not about him, but his Father

In John 20:21 Jesus said, ‘As the father sent me so I send you.’ in other words, we have been called into a life that is as submissive as Jesus.’ To live life as if we have not been sent is to miss an authentic spiritual vocation, reducing our lives to an economic cog in a commercial system, that only exists to generate income.

Do not misunderstand what is being said here, we are not talking about being sent as if it were some big emotional experience where you are called to some ministry or mission location. Rather, being sent is the natural DNA of every child of God, it is living with a continual sense of God’s call upon your life irrespective of the circumstances you may find yourself in. So if you are young or old, male or female, employed or retired, married or single, rich or poor, healthy or ill, if you are a child of God, then you have be sent into your world to continue the mission of Jesus.

Prayer: Father may my life be a continuation of the life and ministry of Jesus.
Bible in a year reading plan: - 2 Kings 14-16
